title: argyranthemum adauctum subsp. gracile
intro: Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. gracile, also known as the Western daisy or Western marguerite, is a flowering plant species native to the Canary Islands and western Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders. Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. gracile is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
